summary: Fresh comprehensive audit of MAXAM after slug-normalization, resources-English-only, and 7-language port

result.summary: Cluster #4 (repo/ops fragility) is essentially UNFIXED and has gotten WORSE since 2026-06-10. The prior audit's #1 immediate action (fix .gitignore, commit scripts + notion-ids.json + the then-5 untracked snapshots + the drift) was never executed: last commit is still 1a03902 (2026-06-10), .gitignore:33 still ignores all of scripts/ (0 scripts tracked), and the count of statically-imported-but-untracked files grew from 5 to 14 (7 product-specs + 7 translations). A fresh clone CANNOT run `astro build` — data.ts and i18n.ts import product-specs.{de,es,fr,it,ja,pt-pt,ru}.json and translations/{de,es,fr,it,ja,pt-pt,ru}.json, all untracked. If this machine dies the entire sync pipeline (129 scripts, 36MB), notion-ids.json (sync's identity map), hand-authored UI translations (90 keys/lang, full parity, irreplaceable), and 7 new product-spec snapshots are lost with no remote copy. Separately, ~918 article block sidecars (~8.6MB) for de/ar-ae/zh-hant are content-identical English duplicates that data.ts never reads (RESOURCE_LANG='en'), and the broader standing decisions on Tailwind and Rubber remain violated (Tailwind still in package.json/astro.config; 17 Rubber* components, 78 files using rubber- classes).
result.stats: git status --porcelain = 470 (437 untracked ?? + 33 modified M). git ls-files scripts = 0. git log -1 = 1a03902 (2026-06-10, "big wip sprint"), zero new commits since prior audit. Tracked files total = 1453. Block sidecars: 1751 on disk, 1328 tracked, 423 untracked. notion-content = 59MB; scripts = 36MB; translations = 84KB. Snapshot product languages = only en/de/ar-ae/zh-hant (4 of 10). 14 statically-imported files untracked. Article-block English duplicate waste ≈ 918 files / 8.6MB.

findings.title: Fresh clone cannot build: 14 statically-imported snapshot/translation files are untracked
findings.evidence: src/lib/data.ts:38-44 imports product-specs.{de,es,fr,it,ja,pt-pt,ru}.json and src/lib/i18n.ts:6-12 imports translations/{de,es,fr,it,ja,pt-pt,ru}.json. `git ls-files --error-unmatch` reports all 14 UNTRACKED while present on disk (e.g. product-specs.de.json 1528938 bytes real data, translations/es.json 90 keys full parity). `git check-ignore` confirms they are NOT ignored — just never `git add`ed. Astro statically imports them, so a clone missing them fails `astro build` at module resolution. Prior audit (docs/AUDIT-2026-06-10.md:115-116) flagged 5 such files; it is now 14.
findings.recommendation: git add the 7 product-specs.<lang>.json and 7 translations/<lang>.json files and commit IMMEDIATELY, before any other work. These are load-bearing build inputs; the translations are hand-authored and not regenerable from Notion.

findings.title: 918 non-English article block files (8.6MB) are byte-identical English duplicates never read
findings.evidence: article-de/ar-ae/zh-hant-* = 918 files 8.6MB; plain_text identical to en; data.ts:526 getArticleBlocks uses RESOURCE_LANG=en (data.ts:508).
findings.recommendation: git rm article-{de,ar-ae,zh-hant}-*; skip non-en sidecars in sync splitBlocks.
findings.verifierNote: CONFIRMED with corrected figures. File count is exact: ls blocks/ shows article-de-* / article-ar-ae-* / article-zh-hant-* = 306 each = 918 non-en article sidecars. 'Never read' is airtight: readBlocks('article',...) has exactly ONE consumer, data.ts:526 'return readBlocks("article", RESOURCE_LANG, slug)', and RESOURCE_LANG='en' (data.ts:508); the page consumer src/pages/[locale]/resources/[slug].astro:30 calls getArticleBlocks(locale,slug) but that ignores locale, so non-en sidecars are unreachable for every locale. TWO overstatements in the evidence: (1) 'byte-identical' is FALSE — for the 301 slugs shared between en and de, 0/301 are byte-identical because block ids, page_ids and timestamps differ (en created 2026-05-03 vs de 2026-06-15); the accurate claim is plain_text(prose)-identical, which holds for 290/301 shared slugs (the other 11 are coincidental slug collisions where en and de point at genuinely different articles, but both bodies are still English). (2) Size: actual apparent bytes for the 918 files = 7.0MB (7,382,191 bytes), not 8.6MB; on-disk du reports only ~1.9M due to 4KB-block rounding of many tiny files. Core waste claim (English prose duplicated across 3 locales, never read) holds; the duplication is of prose content, not literal bytes.

result.summary: The worst prior defect — locale-suffixed URLs and industry-hub/breadcrumb 404s — is genuinely FIXED for the 4 synced locales (en, de, ar-ae, zh-hant). I verified buildCanonicalMaps/canonicalIndustrySlug/canonicalProductSlug against real snapshot data and the dist/ build: every divergent locale slug (tbr-de, rubber-tracks-ar-ae, agricultural-tractor-tires, ms938-con, 33 products + 7 industries) canonicalizes to its English URL, round-trips back to the correct per-locale row, and ships canonical English dirs in dist (no *-de/*-zh-hant dirs). Every LIVE URL builder (RubberHeader, RubberProductCard/IndustryCard/FeaturedCard, both [industry] routes, [slug] breadcrumb) uses urlSlug; the remaining .slug builders are design-gallery-only (env-gated, not shipped). However a NEW critical defect dominates: 6 newly-imported languages (es/fr/it/ja/pt-pt/ru) plus their aliases (es-mx/fr-ca) are fully routable — in the 14-entry language switcher, nav chips, and route tree — but their content was never synced into the snapshot, so the built site has 0 product detail pages and 404 industry hubs for them, and blank homepages. Three prior i18n defects are also still shipping: Simplified Chinese inside zh-hant (verified in dist HTML), zero hreflang, and translationIds consumed by nothing; plus ~918 dead per-locale article block files were newly created.

Build verified: dist/ all 14 locales. 11 findings (3 critical, 5 major, 3 minor).

findings.title: 6 front-end locales (es/fr/it/ja/pt-pt/ru) are routable but have NO catalog content: every product/industry page 404s
findings.evidence: lib/i18n.ts:59-74 lists all 14 locales; every getStaticPaths iterates `locales`. But snapshots only contain en/de/ar-ae/zh-hant: products.json languages = ['ar-ae','de','en','zh-hant'] (117 each), industries.json = same 4, pages.json = en/ar-ae/zh-hant only. Verified in build: `find dist/<l>/products -mindepth 3 -name index.html` = en:112, de:112, ar-ae:112, zh-hant:112, but fr:0, es:0, it:0, ja:0, pt-pt:0, ru:0. `dist/fr/products/tbr-tires/` has NO index.html -> /fr/products/tbr-tires/ is a hard 404; `dist/fr/products/` contains only index.html (empty listing). getAllProducts('fr')=[] and getAllIndustries('fr')=[] (data.ts:399 filters p.language===contentLang('fr')='fr', 0 rows).
findings.recommendation: Either (a) finish Phase C — sync es/fr/it/ja/pt-pt/ru content into the snapshot before these locales are routable, or (b) gate `locales` / route generation to only emit locales that have content in the snapshot (derive the route locale list from languages actually present in products.json), so partially-imported languages don't ship 404 catalogs. The current state ships a switcher that sends users to 404s for 8 of 14 locales' product catalogs.

result.domain: Tailwind removal + CSS (prior audit cluster #1, Tailwind-to-central-CSS migration)
result.summary: The Tailwind removal has NOT advanced since 2026-06-10 and remains ~50% done and fully load-bearing in both directions. `@import 'tailwindcss'` is still global.css line 1, the @theme block still holds ALL ~50 design tokens, the @tailwindcss/vite plugin is still wired in astro.config.mjs, and the packages are still installed. Removing Tailwind today would break everything twice over: (a) every Tailwind utility in 26 .astro files dies, and (b) all 24 central component stylesheets die because they consume 467 var(--color-*/--shadow-*/--radius-*) references that ONLY Tailwind's @theme emits as :root custom properties. The layout/language sprint did NOT regress Tailwind — it added semantic central-CSS classes (home, products hub/detail, Footer fully converted), so a few production surfaces improved; but it also did NOT advance the removal, and the explicitly-flagged blockers (prose-content still undefined, rtl: utilities still load-bearing, contact scoped style block) are untouched. Net: the migration is frozen at the prior audit's state with minor incidental improvement on a handful of pages.
result.stats: Tailwind utility occurrences: ~1785 across 26 of 49 .astro files (prior audit: ~1858 across 26 of 49 — essentially unchanged). global.css unchanged re: Tailwind (only the layout-token region was refactored). @theme = sole source of all design tokens; 467 var(--token) consumptions across all 24 central component stylesheets depend on it. prose-content: referenced on 4 elements, defined in 0 CSS files. rtl:scale-x-[-1]: 12 references across 11 files (8 production-shipping). Scoped <style> blocks: 2 total (contact = production, Specimen = gallery-only). Last commit 1a03902 (2026-06-10); all of the above is uncommitted drift.

Grep for `--color-maxam-blue|--color-surface-page|--shadow-card|--radius-card` outside @theme finds ZERO `:root` fallback definitions — only consumers. The 24 files in src/styles/components/*.css contain 467 `var(--color-*/--shadow-*/--radius-*/--font-size-*/--z-*/--ease-*/--duration-*/--edge-*/--parallax-*/--container-max)` references (header.css:65, button.css:39, chip.css:33, filter-sidebar.css:41, product-card.css:29, …). result.summary: Net direction since 2026-06-10 is split: the token layer genuinely improved, but the page surfaces did not converge. Fixed/improved: a real layout-token system landed in global.css (:root --gutter/--content-edge/--page-gutter + control-height + section-pad scale), the homepage was rebuilt cleanly on Section/Grid plus a sanctioned "wide block" pattern (industries-strip, recent-products) using --page-gutter, and Footer now uses the same --gutter so its content edge finally aligns. Still broken: the three Tailwind pages (resources index, resources detail, contact) were NOT migrated — they still use raw Tailwind containers with a fixed px-4 gutter that does not match --gutter, still import Rubber* components, still carry a scoped <style> block (contact) and the dangling prose-content class (defined nowhere, 4 refs). Container systems were not reduced from the prior count of 5 — they grew to ~6 because the wide --page-gutter system and a new .products-hero banner were added while none of the old Tailwind container variants were removed. Form-atom triplication, the chamfer ::before duplication (~15 sheets), and chip re-implementation outside the component are all still present, and .rubber-* naming is now pervasive in the CSS layer (237 selectors / 18 sheets), plus ~30 lines of newly-dead .products-page__header CSS. New regressions are minor; the dominant story is that the consolidation was applied to home/products/tire only and stopped there.
result.stats: Scope: 30 src CSS files (1 global, 26 component, 3 page) + 9 page .astro + global components. Container/gutter treatments: ~6 distinct (was 5). H1/large-heading treatments: 9+ page-level (.hero-punch, .products-hero__heading, .products-page__heading[dead], .tire-page__title, .strip-heading, .cta-heading, .promo-card__heading, plus raw Tailwind text-3xl/text-4xl h1 on resources×2 + contact, plus dead Hero.astro text-3xl md:text-5xl). Hero/banner systems: 5 (Section bg+overlay; .products-hero; home .hero-content; home promo/cta/strip variants; dead Hero.astro). Section-spacing systems: 2 sanctioned (.section--v-* tokens + --section-pad-* on wide blocks) but Tailwind pages use ad-hoc py-10. Grid implementations: 6 (.grid component, .recent-products__grid, .products-page__grid, .tire-page__docs-grid, .tire-page__hero, .strip-2col) + raw Tailwind grid-cols on resources/contact. .rubber-* selectors: 237 across 18 sheets. Chamfer ::before via --edge-highlight-top: 16 occurrences across 15 sheets + contact inline. Form atoms: 3 near-identical sheets (input/select/textarea). Scoped <style>: 2 (contact, Specimen).

findings.title: Three pages (resources index, resources detail, contact) never migrated off Tailwind containers — parallel gutter system persists
findings.evidence: resources/index.astro:28 `<section class="max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-10">`; contact/index.astro:69 same; resources/[slug].astro:43 `<article class="max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 py-10">`. These use a FIXED `px-4` (1rem) gutter, whereas the sanctioned container .section__container uses `padding-inline: var(--gutter)` = `clamp(1rem, 4vw, 2rem)` (section.css:115, global.css:143). Content edges on these pages do NOT align with home/products/footer at viewports wider than ~1rem-gutter. Violates standing decisions #1 (no Tailwind) and #5 (container/gutter rules).
findings.recommendation: Convert these three pages to BaseLayout content wrapped in `.section`/`.section__container` (or a shared `.page-shell` that consumes --container-max + --gutter), delete the Tailwind container/utility classes. This is the single highest-leverage layout fix remaining.

result.domain: Docs as contract + overall project direction / critical-path sequencing
result.summary: The single highest-leverage prior recommendation — Phase 1, "make CLAUDE.md the contract" — was NOT done: CLAUDE.md is still frozen at the 2026-06-10 prototype-sprint state (it says "Three locales", describes a no-refactor sprint, and contains zero mention of the 14-locale split, resources-English-only, or slug normalization that the team then spent days building). Worse, CLAUDE.md, all of docs/, and all of scripts/ remain gitignored, so the contract document literally cannot be version-controlled. Phase 0 (repo integrity) was also skipped: scripts are still untracked, 14 snapshot/translation files that data.ts/i18n.ts statically import are untracked (fresh clone fails build, up from 5 files in the prior audit), and the entire ~470-file multi-language port sits uncommitted on top of the last commit (2026-06-10) — nothing has been committed in 3+ days of major work. What IS genuinely fixed: the locale-slug 404 cluster (routes/links now use canonical English urlSlug keyed by trid; build is green, 4467 pages) and the locale model split (ContentLanguage 10 / Locale 14 / aliases) are real and well-implemented. What is NEW and concerning: 6 of the new content languages (es/fr/it/ja/pt-pt/ru) plus their aliases ship as empty shells — translated UI chrome and spec files exist, but products.json/articles.json were never synced, so /es/, /fr/, etc. render product catalogs with ZERO products (1 products page vs en's 123), and ~918 non-English article block files are byte-identical English duplicates that no code path ever reads. The team did a wide, shallow language port out of sequence instead of the agreed Phase 0→1 foundation; the result is more surface area, more uncommitted risk, and more divergence between docs and reality — which is exactly the failure mode the prior audit diagnosed.
result.stats: Docs reviewed: CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/AUDIT-2026-06-10.md, docs/LANGUAGE-PARITY-PLAN.md. Code verified: src/types/index.ts, src/lib/i18n.ts, src/lib/data.ts, 3 route files, LanguageSwitcher, contact page, .gitignore. Build: green (exit 0, 4467 pages, 24.6s). Git: HEAD=1a03902 (2026-06-10), 33 modified + 437 untracked = 470 uncommitted; scripts/ docs/ CLAUDE.md = 0 files tracked. Snapshots: products/articles in 4 langs (en/de/ar-ae/zh-hant), specs+translations in all 10. 14 imported files untracked. 1751 block files (918 dead non-en article dupes). 17 Rubber* components / 380 refs; Tailwind still imported, 24 files with utility classes.

CLAUDE.md:18-32 'CURRENT PRIORITY (set 2026-06-10) Prototype sprint... do NOT refactor' is the active priority, yet the team did a large multi-language refactor/port. CLAUDE.md has NO mention of: the locale/contentLang split, LOCALE_ALIASES, urlSlug/slug normalization, or resources-English-only (RESOURCE_LANG, data.ts:508). Prior audit named this exact file 'highest leverage' (AUDIT-2026-06-10.md:165 'Phase 1 — Docs become the contract ... ← highest leverage') and it was not done.

It must describe: the 10/14 locale model + aliases, urlSlug canonicalization, resources-English-only, and the actual current state (6 langs imported to Notion but products/articles NOT synced). Record the resources-English-only and locale-split decisions as standing decisions. This is the single highest-leverage action and it is overdue from the last audit.

findings.verifierNote: CONFIRMED on substance with a precision correction. blocks/ has 1751 files; article files: en=356, ar-ae=306, zh-hant=306, de=306 (1274 total, 918 non-en). data.ts:508 RESOURCE_LANG='en' and getArticleBlocks (data.ts:525-527) calls readBlocks('article', RESOURCE_LANG, slug) — always 'en' — so the 918 non-en article block files are unreachable (getAllArticles/getArticlesByType/etc. all use RESOURCE_LANG, verified data.ts:515-539).
